jobs.title: (80 -100%) R&D HW Engineer
jobs.description:
At ABB, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

This position reports to:

Chapter Lead, Electronics HW

__

In this role, you’ll help run what runs the world, by taking on meaningful work that drives real impact. We are looking for an R&D Senior Hardware Engineer to join our Electrification business. The selected candidate will join a team working on High Power DC converters.

The work model for the role is: #LI-Hybrid

You will be mainly accountable for:

  • Critical member of the Systems and Electrical team, Work within a multidisciplinary team to develop world class mission critical power protection equipment
  • Performs a variety of complex technology and engineering assignments
  • Realize the design of architecture and technical details, the circuit simulation and the development of electronic projects which require knowledge of analog, digital and low power electronics
  • Take care of design optimization by identification of technical, architectural and circuitry solutions, exploiting the highest integration (mechanical part, electrical components, PCBAs, etc.) and collaborating with other departments involved in the Product Development Process
  • Carry out preliminary tests for design pre-validation
  • Support the Test Lab during all the testing phases, evaluate reports of Test Lab and Quality Laboratories and express your definitive opinion on validation of final product
  • Draw up the technical documentation of systems, components, and interfaces

Our Team Dynamics

Our teams support each other, collaborate, and never stop learning. Everyone brings something unique, and together we push ideas forward to solve real problems. Being part of our team means your work matters - because the progress we make here creates real impact out there.

Qualifications for the role:

  • Bachelor or Master’s degree in Electronics or Electrical Engineering
  • 5+ years' experience with electrical systems and complex power electronic systems.
  • Experience in digital and analog circuit design and simulation.
  • Experience with EMI design and troubleshooting
  • Knowledge of environmental, reliability, and safety requirements for Industrial products.
  • Knowledge and experience with Agile methodology is a plus.
  • Excellent command of English

More about us

ABB Smart Power provides energy distribution solutions for data centers, industrial and manufacturing plants, critical infrastructure and commercial buildings. The Division’s technical teams work closely with industry partners, delivering advanced solutions that support rapid growth, energy transition, and sustainability objectives. The Division’s portfolio includes industrial circuit breakers, low-voltage systems, motor starting applications, and safety devices like switches and relays. Its Critical Power unit supports the world’s largest data center companies with advanced energy-efficient UPS solutions. The Division’s ABB Ability™ Energy Manager provides a scalable, easy-to-use platform that helps organizations save energy and reduce CO2 emissions.

"If you want to discover more about ABB, take another look at our website www.abb.com. ABB Data Privacy Statement: https://new.abb.com/privacy-notice/candidate"

Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences.

Ready to make an impact?

Apply today or visit https://www.abb.com to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

descriptionByLocale.it: ABB , aiutiamo le industrie a gestire più snella e più pulita, e ogni persona qui lo fa accadere. Sarete autorizzati a guidare, sostenuto per crescere, e orgoglioso dell'impatto che creiamo insieme. Unisciti a noi e aiuta a gestire ciò che gestisce il mondo. Questa posizione riporta a: Capitolo Piombo, Elettronica HW    In questo ruolo, vi aiuterà a eseguire ciò che gestisce il mondo, assumendo un lavoro significativo che guida l'impatto reale. Stiamo cercando un ingegnere hardware senior R&D per unirsi al nostro business di Elettrificazione. Il candidato selezionato si unirà a un team che lavora ai convertitori High Power DC. Il modello di lavoro per il ruolo è: #LI-Hybrid Sarete principalmente responsabili per: - Membro critico del team Sistemi ed Elettrici, Lavoro all'interno di un team multidisciplinare per sviluppare le apparecchiature di protezione della potenza critica di classe mondiale - Esegue una varietà di complessi incarichi tecnologici e ingegneristici - Realizzare la progettazione di architettura e dettagli tecnici, la simulazione del circuito e lo sviluppo di progetti elettronici che richiedono la conoscenza di elettronica analogica, digitale e bassa potenza - Prendere cura dell'ottimizzazione della progettazione mediante l'identificazione di soluzioni tecniche, architettoniche e circuitali, sfruttando la massima integrazione (parte meccanica, componenti elettrici, PCBA, ecc.) e collaborando con altri reparti coinvolti nel processo di sviluppo del prodotto - Eseguire test preliminari per la progettazione pre-valida - Sostenere il Laboratorio di prova durante tutte le fasi di test, valutare i report di Laboratori e laboratori di qualità ed esprimere il vostro parere definitivo sulla convalida del prodotto finale - I nostri team si sostengono, collaborano e non smettono mai di imparare. Tutti portano qualcosa di unico, e insieme spingiamo avanti idee per risolvere problemi reali. Essere parte del nostro team significa che il vostro lavoro conta - perché il progresso che facciamo qui crea un impatto reale là fuori. Qualifiche per il ruolo: - Laurea o Master in Elettronica o Ingegneria Elettrica - 5+ anni di esperienza con sistemi elettrici e sistemi elettronici di potenza complessi. - Esperienza nella progettazione e nella simulazione di circuiti digitali e analogici. - Esperienza con la progettazione e la risoluzione dei problemi EMI - Conoscenza dei requisiti ambientali, di affidabilità e di sicurezza per i prodotti industriali. - La conoscenza e l'esperienza con la metodologia Agile è un vantaggio. ABB Smart Power offre soluzioni di distribuzione energetica per data center, impianti industriali e produttivi, infrastrutture critiche e edifici commerciali. I team tecnici della Divisione lavorano a stretto contatto con i partner del settore, offrendo soluzioni avanzate che supportano obiettivi di crescita rapida, transizione energetica e sostenibilità. Il portafoglio della Divisione comprende interruttori industriali, sistemi a bassa tensione, applicazioni di avviamento motore e dispositivi di sicurezza come interruttori e relè. La sua unità Critical Power supporta le più grandi aziende di data center del mondo con soluzioni UPS ad alta efficienza energetica. ABB AbilityTM Energy Manager offre una piattaforma scalabile e facile da usare che consente alle organizzazioni di risparmiare energia e ridurre le emissioni di CO2. "Se volete saperne di più su ABB, date un'altra occhiata al nostro sito www.abb.com. ABB Informativa sulla privacy dei dati: https://new.abb.com/privacy-notice/candidate " Costruire un futuro più pulito e più intelligente prende tutti i tipi di menti: il curioso, il coraggioso e il creativo. Ecco perché accogliamo le persone da tutti gli sfondi e le esperienze. Pronti a fare un impatto? Applicare o visitare https://www.abb.com per saperne di più sull'impatto delle nostre soluzioni in tutto il mondo.
descriptionByLocale.de: Bei ABB helfen wir Industrien, schlanker und sauberer zu laufen – und jeder Mensch macht das hier. Sie werden ermächtigt, zu führen, unterstützt zu wachsen und stolz auf die Auswirkungen, die wir zusammen schaffen. Begleiten Sie uns und helfen Sie zu laufen, was die Welt führt. Diese Position berichtet an: Chapter Lead, Electronics HW    In dieser Rolle helfen Sie, das zu führen, was die Welt führt, indem Sie auf sinnvolle Arbeit, die wirkliche Auswirkungen treibt. Wir suchen einen R&D Senior Hardware Engineer, der sich unserem Electrification-Geschäft anschließt. Der ausgewählte Kandidat wird sich einem Team an High Power DC-Konvertern anschließen. Das Arbeitsmodell für die Rolle ist: #LI-Hybrid Sie sind vor allem verantwortlich für: - Critical Member of the Systems and Electrical team, Arbeit innerhalb eines multidisziplinären Teams, um Weltklasse-Mission kritische Leistungsschutzausrüstung zu entwickeln - Führen Sie eine Vielzahl von komplexen Technologie- und Engineering-Beauftragungen - Realisieren Sie das Design von Architektur und technischen Details, die Schaltungssimulation und die Entwicklung von elektronischen Projekten, die Kenntnisse der analogen, digitalen und niedrigen Leistungselektronik erfordern - Achten Sie auf die Designoptimierung durch die Identifikation von technischen, architektonischen und schaltungstechnischen Lösungen, die Nutzung der höchsten Integration (mechanischer Teil, elektrische Komponenten, PCBAs, etc.) und die Zusammenarbeit mit anderen Abteilungen, die am Produktentwicklungsprozess beteiligt sind - Durchführung von Vorprüfungen für Designvorvalidierung - Unterstützung des Testlabors in allen Testphasen, Bewertung von Berichten von Testlabor und Quality Laboratories und drücken Sie Ihre endgültige Meinung zur Validierung von Endprodukten - Unsere Teams unterstützen einander, kooperieren und nie aufhören zu lernen. Jeder bringt etwas Einzigartiges, und gemeinsam drängen wir Ideen, um echte Probleme zu lösen. Ein Teil unseres Teams bedeutet, dass Ihre Arbeit wichtig ist - denn der Fortschritt, den wir hier machen, schafft wirkliche Auswirkungen. Qualifikationen für die Rolle: - Bachelor oder Master-Abschluss in Elektronik oder Elektrotechnik - 5 Jahre Erfahrung mit elektrischen Systemen und komplexen Stromelektroniksystemen. - Erfahrung in der digitalen und analogen Schaltungsgestaltung und -simulation. - Erfahrung mit EMI Design und Fehlerbehebung - Kenntnisse der Umwelt-, Zuverlässigkeits- und Sicherheitsanforderungen für Industrieprodukte. - Wissen und Erfahrung mit der Agile-Methodik ist ein Plus. - Ausgezeichneter Befehl von Englisch Mehr über uns ABB Smart Power bietet Energieverteilungslösungen für Rechenzentren, Industrie- und Fertigungsanlagen, kritische Infrastruktur und kommerzielle Gebäude. Die technischen Teams der Division arbeiten eng mit Industriepartnern zusammen und liefern fortschrittliche Lösungen, die schnelles Wachstum, Energiewende und Nachhaltigkeitsziele unterstützen. Das Portfolio der Division umfasst Industrieschaltungsschalter, Niederspannungssysteme, Motorstartanwendungen und Sicherheitseinrichtungen wie Schalter und Relais. Das Critical Power Unit unterstützt die weltweit größten Rechenzentrumsunternehmen mit fortschrittlichen energieeffizienten UPS-Lösungen. Der ABB AbilityTM Energy Manager der Division bietet eine skalierbare, einfach zu bedienende Plattform, die Unternehmen dabei unterstützt, Energie zu sparen und CO2-Emissionen zu reduzieren. "Wenn Sie mehr über ABB erfahren möchten, werfen Sie einen weiteren Blick auf unsere Website www.abb.com. ABB Datenschutzerklärung: https://new.abb.com/privacy-notice/candidate "Die Schaffung einer saubereren, intelligenteren Zukunft nimmt alle Arten von Gedanken: die Neugierige, die Mutigen und die Kreativen. Deshalb begrüßen wir Menschen aus allen Hintergründen und Erfahrungen. Bereit, einen Einfluss zu machen? Wenden Sie sich heute an oder besuchen Sie https://www.abb.com, um mehr über die Auswirkungen unserer Lösungen auf der ganzen Welt zu erfahren.
descriptionByLocale.fr: A l'ABB, nous aidons les industries à fonctionner de façon plus propre, et chaque personne ici fait que cela arrive. Vous aurez le pouvoir de diriger, de soutenir pour grandir, et fier de l'impact que nous créons ensemble. Rejoignez-nous et aidez à gérer ce qui dirige le monde. Cette position se rapporte à : Chapitre plomb, électronique HW    Dans ce rôle, vous allez aider à exécuter ce qui dirige le monde, en prenant un travail significatif qui entraîne un impact réel. Nous sommes à la recherche d'un ingénieur principal en matériel de R-D pour rejoindre notre entreprise d'électrification. Le candidat sélectionné se joindra à une équipe travaillant sur les convertisseurs à courant continu à haute puissance. Le modèle de travail pour le rôle est: #LI-Hybrid Vous serez principalement responsable de : - Membre critique de l'équipe Systèmes et Électricité, Travaillez au sein d'une équipe multidisciplinaire pour développer des équipements de protection de puissance critiques de classe mondiale de mission - Effectue une variété de tâches technologiques et techniques complexes - Réaliser la conception de l'architecture et des détails techniques, la simulation de circuit et le développement de projets électroniques qui nécessitent une connaissance de l'électronique analogique, numérique et de faible puissance - Prendre soin de l'optimisation de la conception en identifiant des solutions techniques, architecturales et de circuits, en exploitant la plus haute intégration (partie mécanique, composants électriques, PCBA, etc.) et en collaborant avec d'autres départements impliqués dans le processus de développement du produit - Effectuer des tests préliminaires pour la prévalidation de la conception - Soutenir le laboratoire d'essai pendant toutes les phases d'essai, évaluer les rapports du laboratoire d'essai et des laboratoires de qualité et exprimer votre avis définitif sur la validation du produit final - Élaborer la documentation technique des systèmes, composants et interfaces Notre équipe Dynamique Nos équipes se soutiennent, collaborent et n'arrêtent jamais d'apprendre. Tout le monde apporte quelque chose d'unique, et ensemble nous faisons avancer les idées pour résoudre de vrais problèmes. Faire partie de notre équipe signifie que votre travail compte - parce que les progrès que nous faisons ici créent un impact réel là-bas. Qualifications pour le rôle: - Baccalauréat ou maîtrise en électronique ou en génie électrique - 5+ ans d'expérience avec les systèmes électriques et les systèmes électroniques de puissance complexes. - Expérience en conception et simulation de circuits numériques et analogiques. - Expérience de la conception et du dépannage EMI - Connaissance des exigences environnementales, de fiabilité et de sécurité des produits industriels. - Connaissance et expérience de la méthodologie Agile est un plus. - Excellente maîtrise de l'anglais Plus sur nous ABB Smart Power fournit des solutions de distribution d'énergie pour les datacenters, les usines industrielles et manufacturières, les infrastructures critiques et les bâtiments commerciaux. Les équipes techniques de la Division travaillent en étroite collaboration avec les partenaires de l'industrie, fournissant des solutions avancées qui soutiennent la croissance rapide, la transition énergétique et les objectifs de durabilité. Le portefeuille de la Division comprend des disjoncteurs industriels, des systèmes à basse tension, des applications de démarrage de moteurs et des dispositifs de sécurité comme les commutateurs et les relais. Son unité Critical Power prend en charge les plus grandes entreprises de centres de données du monde avec des solutions UPS à haut rendement énergétique. La division ABB CapabilityTM Energy Manager fournit une plateforme évolutive et facile à utiliser qui aide les organisations à économiser de l'énergie et à réduire les émissions de CO2. "Si vous voulez en savoir plus sur ABB, consultez notre site www.abb.com. ABB Data Privacy Statement: https://new.abb.com/privacy-notice/candidate " Construire un avenir plus propre et plus intelligent prend toutes sortes d'esprits: les curieux, les courageux et les créatifs. C'est pourquoi nous accueillons des gens de tous horizons et expériences. Prêt à avoir un impact ? Faites une demande aujourd'hui ou visitez https://www.abb.com pour en savoir plus sur l'impact de nos solutions à travers le monde.

jobs.title: (80 - 100%) R&D Engineer (Energy Storage Systems)
jobs.description:
At ABB, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

This position reports to:

Chapter Lead, Electronics HW

__

You will utilize your extensive skills and technical expertise to select and qualify potential Energy Storage Systems and their manufacturers, ensure Uninterruptable Power Supplies (UPS) compatibility with those. You will further support us with new emerging applications around multifunction UPS for ancillary services such as frequency response and peak shaving.

The work model for the role is: #LI-Hybrid

You will be mainly accountable for:

  • Lead technical qualification of Energy Storage Systems (eg. battery cells, battery racks, Battery Management Systems, etc.)
  • Support supplier qualification
  • Support certification of batteries and systems for the American and IEC standards
  • Support on new emerging applications and technical know-how on batteries
  • Identify and communicate energy storage requirements

Qualifications for the role:

  • Master or Bachelor degree in Electrical Engineering, as well as more than 3 years of relevant Engineering experience
  • Must have experience working with electrical systems or demonstrate the aptitude to learn complex power electronic systems
  • Must have experience with energy storage systems and batteries
  • Self-driven and responsible, well-structured work practices
  • Can break down complex topics into simple explanations
  • Collaborative team player
  • Knowledge and experience with Agile methodology is a plus
  • Fluent in English, spoken and written

More about us

ABB Smart Power provides energy distribution solutions for data centers, industrial and manufacturing plants, critical infrastructure and commercial buildings. The Division’s technical teams work closely with industry partners, delivering advanced solutions that support rapid growth, energy transition, and sustainability objectives. The Division’s portfolio includes industrial circuit breakers, low-voltage systems, motor starting applications, and safety devices like switches and relays. Its Critical Power unit supports the world’s largest data center companies with advanced energy-efficient UPS solutions. The Division’s ABB Ability™ Energy Manager provides a scalable, easy-to-use platform that helps organizations save energy and reduce CO2 emissions.

jobs.title: (80 -100%) R&D Senior Engineer (Energy Storage Systems)
jobs.description:
At ABB, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

This position reports to:

Chapter Lead, Electronics HW

__

You will utilize your extensive skills and technical expertise to select and qualify potential Energy Storage Systems and their manufacturers, ensure Uninterruptable Power Supplies (UPS) compatibility with those. You will further support us with new emerging applications around multifunction UPS for ancillary services such as frequency response and peak shaving.

The work model for the role is: #LI-Hybrid

You will be mainly accountable for:

  • Lead technical qualification of Energy Storage Systems (eg. battery cells, battery racks, Battery Management Systems, etc.)
  • Support supplier qualification
  • Stakeholders to identify potential new battery technologies
  • Support certification of batteries and systems for the American and IEC standards
  • Support on new emerging applications and technical know-how on batteries
  • Identify and communicate energy storage requirements

Qualifications for the role:

  • Master’s or Bachelor’s degree in Science, Technology, Engineering, as well as more than 5 years of relevant Engineering experience
  • Must have experience working with electrical systems or demonstrate the aptitude to learn complex power electronic systems
  • Must have experience with energy storage systems and batteries
  • Self-driven and responsible, well-structured work practices
  • Can break down complex topics into simple explanations
  • Collaborative team player
  • Knowledge and experience with Agile methodology is a plus
  • Fluent in English, spoken and written

More about us

ABB Smart Power provides energy distribution solutions for data centers, industrial and manufacturing plants, critical infrastructure and commercial buildings. The Division’s technical teams work closely with industry partners, delivering advanced solutions that support rapid growth, energy transition, and sustainability objectives. The Division’s portfolio includes industrial circuit breakers, low-voltage systems, motor starting applications, and safety devices like switches and relays. Its Critical Power unit supports the world’s largest data center companies with advanced energy-efficient UPS solutions. The Division’s ABB Ability™ Energy Manager provides a scalable, easy-to-use platform that helps organizations save energy and reduce CO2 emissions.

"If you want to discover more about ABB, take another look at our website www.abb.com. ABB Data Privacy Statement: https://new.abb.com/privacy-notice/candidate"

Ready to make an impact?

jobs.title: (80 - 100 %) Scrum Master
jobs.description:
At ABB, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

This position reports to:

Chapter Lead

__

Electrification provides leading electrical distribution and management technologies, solutions and services to electrify the world in a safe, smart and sustainable way. The portfolio includes medium- and low-voltage electrical components, switchgear, digital devices, enclosures, and circuit breakers, among others. With our products, solutions and services, we collaborate with customers to improve power delivery and security, enhance energy management, efficiency and operational reliability, as we seek to achieve a low carbon society. go.abb/electrification.

In this role you will be part in pioneering a new way of working within our Smart Power Division. You will implement and lead the Agile practices at team level for one or more teams. You will guide on the Agile principles, helping the team to achieve valuable outcomes, and identify continuous improvement, bringing a data driven approach in day-to-day management.

The work model for the role is: hybrid #LI-hybrid

You will be mainly accountable for:

  • Guide and coach Scrum teams in applying Agile practices and principles, fostering positive team dynamics, collaboration, and continuous improvement
  • Develop high‑performing teams by identifying strengths and areas for growth, using effective coaching techniques, and promoting empowering Agile behaviors
  • Facilitate key Scrum events (Sprint Planning, Daily Scrum, Sprint Review, Sprint Retrospective) to ensure engagement, transparency, and smooth delivery
  • Strengthen Agile maturity across the organization by building cross-team alliances, improving Agile practices, and ensuring adherence to Scrum theory, practices, and guidelines

Qualifications for the role:

  • Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent (this position is not embedded in the IT environment; we are open for profiles with different backgrounds like psychology, social science, engineering, business administration, economics etc.)
  • 5 or more years of experience as Scrum Master or in a similar Agile role applying Agile principles and practices is an advantage
  • Fluent command of English with Italian considered as additional advantage
  • Motivated to work in an Agile, dynamic workplace and cross functional team
  • Experience in leading people with the ability to earn trust while challenging the team to improve and operate outside its comfort zone
  • Strong creative problem – solving skills and the ability to get things done
  • Excellent communication, facilitation, negotiation, coaching, risk management and conflict-resolution skills, with the ability to foster positive team dynamics and collaboration

Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences.

Ready to make an impact?

Apply today or visit https://www.abb.com to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

Le modèle de travail pour le rôle est : hybride #LI-hybrid ## Mansioni - Guide et coach Les équipes Scrum dans l'application des pratiques et des principes Agiles, favorisant la dynamique positive de l'équipe, la collaboration et l'amélioration continue - Développer des équipes hautement performantes en identifiant les forces et les domaines de croissance, en utilisant des techniques efficaces de coaching, et en favorisant l'autonomisation des comportements Agiles - Faciliter les principaux événements Scrum s (Planification Sprint, Daily Scrum, Sprint Review, Sprint Retrospective) afin d'assurer l'engagement, la transparence et l'exécution sans heurts - Renforcer la maturité Agile dans l'organisation en construisant des alliances entre équipes, en améliorant les pratiques Agiles, et en veillant à l'adhésion à la théorie, aux pratiques et aux lignes directrices de Scrum ## Requisiti - Baccalauréat Degré ou équivalent (ce poste n'est pas intégré dans l'environnement de TI; nous sommes ouverts à des profils avec différents horizons comme la psychologie, les sciences sociales, l'ingénierie, l'administration des affaires, l'économie, etc.) - 5 ans ou plus d' Faites une demande aujourd'hui ou visitez https://www.abb.com pour en savoir plus sur l'impact de nos solutions à travers le monde.

jobs.title: Shift Leader Collaudo IPU Modular (80-100%)
jobs.description:
At ABB, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

This position reports to:

Production Supervisor

__

Job Summary

Contributes to ensuring a safe working environment in compliance with company guidelines, guaranteeing the achievement of Production targets during the shift. Supports the Production Supervisors in the daily coordination of operational activities, providing technical assistance to operators in the testing areas.

Your Roles and Responsibilities

  • Monitoring and ensuring compliance with ABB safety guidelines and Code of Conduct during the shift

  • Supporting the Production Supervisor to ensure the correct execution of production activities

  • Providing technical support to operators (Testing) during daily operations

  • Acting as the reference point for on‑the‑job training of production personnel

  • Serving as the first level of contact for operational issues to be escalated to the relevant Production Supervisor

  • Participating in Level 1 Q Circles: gathers technical and quality issues and communicating them to the appropriate supervisor

The work model for this role is on-site: #LI-Onsite

License to Operate / Core Values

  • Ensuring that all personnel (within the area of responsibility) understand ABB’s core values and the group’s safety and integrity standards, and act accordingly

  • Implementing and ensuring compliance with ABB’s Code of Conduct, tools, policies, and processes within the assigned area

Collaboration / Leadership

  • Promoting and enabling collaboration within the relevant work areas

  • Managing relationships with stakeholders in the area of responsibility and ensures alignment

Professional Skills and Experience

  • Minimum 2–3 years of experience in operations, with experience in coordination roles

  • Experience in the electrical/electronic field

  • Previous experience in team coordination roles is considered a plus

Education

  • Vocational school diploma or equivalent

Please note that applications and CVs should be submitted in English.

More About Us

ABB Smart Power provides energy distribution solutions for data centers, industrial and manufacturing plants, critical infrastructure and commercial buildings. The Division’s technical teams work closely with industry partners, delivering advanced solutions that support rapid growth, energy transition, and sustainability objectives. The Division’s portfolio includes industrial circuit breakers, low-voltage systems, motor starting applications, and safety devices like switches and relays. Its Power Protection unit supports the world’s largest data center companies with advanced energy-efficient UPS solutions. The Division’s ABB Ability™ Energy Manager provides a scalable, easy-to-use platform that helps organizations save energy and reduce CO2 emissions.

The recruitment process is managed by Mara Werne, Talent Partner.

Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences.

Ready to make an impact?

Apply today or visit https://www.abb.com to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

descriptionByLocale.de: Bei ABB helfen wir Industrien, schlanker und sauberer zu laufen – und jeder Mensch macht das hier. Sie werden ermächtigt, zu führen, unterstützt zu wachsen, und stolz auf die Auswirkungen, die wir zusammen schaffen. Begleiten Sie uns und helfen Sie zu laufen, was die Welt führt. Diese Position berichtet an: Produktionsaufsicht    Job Zusammenfassung trägt dazu bei, ein sicheres Arbeitsumfeld in Übereinstimmung mit den Unternehmensleitlinien zu gewährleisten und die Erreichung der Produktionsziele während der Schicht zu gewährleisten. Unterstützt die Produktionsaufsichtsbehörden bei der täglichen Koordinierung der operativen Tätigkeiten und bietet den Betreibern in den Prüfbereichen technische Hilfe. Ihre Rollen und Verantwortungen - Überwachung und Sicherstellung der Einhaltung der ABB-Sicherheitsrichtlinien und Verhaltenskodex während der Verschiebung - Unterstützung der Produktionsaufsicht, um die korrekte Ausführung der Produktionsaktivitäten sicherzustellen - Bereitstellung technischer Unterstützung für die Betreiber (Testen) während des täglichen Betriebs - Als Bezugspunkt für die On-the-Job-Ausbildung des Produktionspersonals fungieren - dienen als erstes Kontaktniveau für operationelle Fragen, um sie auf die jeweilige Produktionsaufsichtsebene zu beschränken - Das Arbeitsmodell für diese Rolle ist vor Ort: #LI-Onsite License to Operate / Core Values - Sicherstellung, dass alle Mitarbeiter (innerhalb des Verantwortungsbereichs) ABB die Kernwerte und die Sicherheits- und Integritätsstandards der Gruppe verstehen und entsprechend handeln - Umsetzung und Sicherstellung der Einhaltung von ABB Professional Code of Conduct, Tools, Richtlinien und Prozesse innerhalb des zugewiesenen Bereichs Mehr Über uns ABB Smart Power bietet Energieverteilungslösungen für Rechenzentren, Industrie- und Fertigungsanlagen, kritische Infrastrukturen und kommerzielle Gebäude. Die technischen Teams der Division arbeiten eng mit Industriepartnern zusammen und liefern fortschrittliche Lösungen, die schnelles Wachstum, Energiewende und Nachhaltigkeitsziele unterstützen. Das Portfolio der Division umfasst Industrieschaltungsschalter, Niederspannungssysteme, Motorstartanwendungen und Sicherheitseinrichtungen wie Schalter und Relais. Die Power Protection Unit unterstützt die weltweit größten Rechenzentrumsunternehmen mit fortschrittlichen energieeffizienten UPS-Lösungen. Der ABB AbilityTM Energy Manager der Division bietet eine skalierbare, einfach zu bedienende Plattform, die Unternehmen dabei unterstützt, Energie zu sparen und CO2-Emissionen zu reduzieren. Der Rekrutierungsprozess wird von Mara Werne, Talent Partner, geleitet. Die Schaffung einer saubereren, intelligenteren Zukunft nimmt alle Arten von Gedanken: die neugierig, mutig, und die kreative. Deshalb begrüßen wir Menschen aus allen Hintergründen und Erfahrungen. Bereit, einen Einfluss zu machen? Wenden Sie sich heute an oder besuchen Sie https://www.abb.com, um mehr über die Auswirkungen unserer Lösungen auf der ganzen Welt zu erfahren.
descriptionByLocale.fr: Chez ABB , nous aidons les industries à fonctionner plus minces et plus propres, et chaque personne ici fait que cela arrive. Vous aurez le pouvoir de diriger, de soutenir la croissance et d'être fiers de l'impact que nous créons ensemble. Rejoignez-nous et aidez à gérer ce qui dirige le monde. Ce poste relève de : Superviseur de la production    Résumé de l'emploi Contribue à assurer un environnement de travail sécuritaire conformément aux lignes directrices de l'entreprise, garantissant la réalisation des objectifs de production pendant le quart de travail. Soutient les superviseurs de la production dans la coordination quotidienne des activités opérationnelles, fournissant une assistance technique aux opérateurs dans les zones d'essais. Vos rôles et responsabilités - Surveiller et assurer le respect des lignes directrices et du code de conduite en matière de sécurité des ABB pendant le quart de travail - Aider le superviseur de la production à assurer la bonne exécution des activités de production - Fournir un soutien technique aux exploitants (test) pendant les opérations quotidiennes - Agir comme point de référence pour la formation en cours d'emploi du personnel de production - Servir de premier niveau de contact pour les questions opérationnelles à augmenter au superviseur de la production pertinent - Participer aux cercles Q de niveau 1 : recueillir les questions techniques et de qualité et les communiquer au superviseur approprié Le modèle de travail pour ce rôle est sur place : #LI-License onsite to Operate / Core Values - S'assurer que tout le personnel (dans le domaine de responsabilité) comprend les valeurs fondamentales de l'ABB et les normes de sécurité et d'intégrité du groupe, et agir en conséquence - Mettre en oeuvre et assurer la conformité avec le code de conduite, les outils, les politiques et les processus de l'ABB dans le domaine de responsabilité Collaboration / Leadership - Promouvoir et faciliter la collaboration dans les domaines de travail pertinents - Gérer les relations avec les intervenants dans le domaine de responsabilité et assurer l'alignement Compétences et expérience professionnelles - Minimum de 2 à 3 ans d'expérience dans les opérations, avec une expérience dans les rôles de coordination - Expérience dans le domaine de l'électricité et de l'électronique - Expérience antérieure dans les rôles de coordination d'équipe est considéré comme un diplôme d'études professionnelles ou équivalent. Plus À propos de nous ABB Smart Power fournit des solutions de distribution d'énergie pour les centres de données, les usines industrielles et manufacturières, les infrastructures essentielles et les bâtiments commerciaux. Les équipes techniques de la Division travaillent en étroite collaboration avec des partenaires de l'industrie, fournissant des solutions avancées qui soutiennent la croissance rapide, la transition énergétique et les objectifs de durabilité. Le portefeuille de la Division comprend des disjoncteurs industriels, des systèmes à basse tension, des applications de démarrage de moteurs et des dispositifs de sécurité comme les commutateurs et les relais. Son unité de protection de l'énergie prend en charge les plus grandes entreprises de centres de données du monde avec des solutions UPS à haut rendement énergétique. La division ABB CapabilityTM Energy Manager fournit une plateforme évolutive et facile à utiliser qui aide les organisations à économiser de l'énergie et à réduire les émissions de CO2. Le processus de recrutement est géré par Mara Werne, Talent Partner. Construire un avenir plus propre et plus intelligent prend toutes sortes d'esprits : les curieux, les courageux et les créatifs. C'est pourquoi nous accueillons des gens de tous horizons et expériences. Prêt à faire un impact ? Faites une demande aujourd'hui ou visitez https://www.abb.com pour en savoir plus sur l'impact de nos solutions à travers le monde.

jobs.title: Shift leader Assemblaggio / Imballo (80-100%)
jobs.description:
At ABB, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

This position reports to:

Production Supervisor

__

Job Summary

Contributes to ensuring a safe working environment in compliance with company guidelines, guaranteeing the achievement of Production targets during the shift. Supports the Production Supervisors in the daily coordination of operational activities, providing technical assistance to operators in the assembly and packaging areas.

The work model for this role is on-site: #LI-Onsite

Main Roles and Responsibilities

  • Monitoring and ensuring compliance with ABB safety guidelines and Code of Conduct during the shift

  • Supporting the Production Supervisor / Logistics Supervisor to ensure the correct execution of production activities

  • Providing technical support to operators (Assembly, Packaging) during daily operations

  • Acting as the reference point for on‑the‑job training of production personnel

  • Serving as the first level of contact for operational issues to be escalated to the relevant Production Supervisor

  • Participating in Level 1 Q Circles by gathering technical and quality issues and communicating them to the appropriate supervisor

License to Operate / Core Values

  • Ensuring that all personnel (within the area of responsibility) understand ABB’s core values and the group’s safety and integrity standards, and acting accordingly

  • Implementing and ensuring compliance with ABB’s Code of Conduct, tools, policies, and processes within the assigned area

Collaboration / Leadership

  • Promoting and enabling collaboration within the relevant work areas

  • Managing relationships with stakeholders in the area of responsibility and ensures alignment

Professional Skills and Experience

  • Minimum 2–3 years of experience in operations, with experience in coordination roles

  • Experience in the electromechanical field

  • Previous experience in team coordination roles is considered a plus

Education

  • Vocational school diploma or equivalent

Please note that applications and CVs should be submitted in English.

More About Us

ABB Smart Power provides energy distribution solutions for data centers, industrial and manufacturing plants, critical infrastructure and commercial buildings. The Division’s technical teams work closely with industry partners, delivering advanced solutions that support rapid growth, energy transition, and sustainability objectives. The Division’s portfolio includes industrial circuit breakers, low-voltage systems, motor starting applications, and safety devices like switches and relays. Its Power Protection unit supports the world’s largest data center companies with advanced energy-efficient UPS solutions. The Division’s ABB Ability™ Energy Manager provides a scalable, easy-to-use platform that helps organizations save energy and reduce CO2 emissions.

The recruitment process is managed by Mara Werne, Talent Partner.

Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences.

Ready to make an impact?

Apply today or visit https://www.abb.com to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

jobs.title: Quality Technician (80-100%)
jobs.description:
At ABB, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

This position reports to:

Quality Manager

__

Il tuo ruolo e le tue responsabilità:

In questo ruolo, contribuirai all'implementazione della nostra strategia di qualità in tutta l'unità organizzativa locale, promuovendo una forte cultura della qualità e implementando sistemi efficaci, strumenti e team qualificati, tutti incentrati sul superamento delle aspettative dei clienti e sul mantenimento di operazioni sostenibili. Ogni giorno, bilancerai prevenzione, miglioramento continuo e risposta rapida alle sfide di qualità, collaborando con team interfunzionali per migliorare i risultati di qualità. Il tuo lavoro supporterà direttamente l'unità organizzativa nel raggiungimento degli obiettivi di performance e nel progresso verso standard di livello mondiale.

Il modello di lavoro per questo ruolo è in sede: #LI-Onsite #Work-Shifts

Questo ruolo contribuisce alla divisione Critical Power, Smart Power presso Quartino, Svizzera.

Sarai principalmente responsabile di:

  • Supportare attivamente le linee di produzione come primo punto di contatto per le questioni relative alla qualità.
  • Tracciare e registrare sistematicamente tutte le non conformità generate dalle linee di produzione.
  • Contribuire al miglioramento degli indicatori chiave di qualità, principalmente il First Pass Yield (FPY) e il costo complessivo della scarsa qualità (COPQ).
  • Segnalare tempestivamente e accuratamente le non conformità al fornitore.
  • Controllare, implementare e migliorare continuamente i controlli di qualità per migliorare la qualità dei prodotti e il controllo dei processi.
  • Gestire il magazzino dei prodotti non conformi e lavorare attivamente per ridurre i livelli di inventario.

Requisiti per il ruolo:

  • Tecnico meccanico o elettrico o settore correlato.
  • Minimo 2 anni di esperienza in ruoli relativi alla qualità o equivalenti.
  • Profonda conoscenza dei processi di qualità, comprese le metodologie di risoluzione dei problemi e gli strumenti di miglioramento continuo.
  • Capacità comunicative e interpersonali con la capacità di lavorare efficacemente con gli stakeholder a tutti i livelli organizzativi.
  • Disponibilità a lavorare su turni e oltre l'orario standard quando richiesto, e capacità di lavorare in modo efficiente in situazioni impegnative.
  • La lingua principale è l'italiano, scritto e parlato.
  • La conoscenza dell'inglese è considerata un vantaggio.

Si prega di notare che le candidature e i CV devono essere inviati in inglese.

Maggiori informazioni su di noi

ABB Smart Power fornisce soluzioni di distribuzione dell'energia per data center, impianti di produzione, infrastrutture critiche ed edifici commerciali. I team tecnici della Divisione lavorare a stretto contatto con i partner del settore, fornendo soluzioni avanzate che supportano la rapida crescita, transizione e gli obiettivi di sostenibilità. Il portafoglio della Divisione comprende interruttori industriali, sistemi a bassa tensione, applicazioni di avviamento motore e dispositivi di sicurezza come interruttori e relè. La sua unità Power Protection supporta le più grandi aziende di data center del mondo con soluzioni UPS avanzate ed efficienti dal punto di vista energetico. ABB Ability™ Energy Manager della Divisione fornisce una piattaforma scalabile e facile da usare che aiuta le organizzazioni a risparmiare energia e ridurre le emissioni di CO2.

Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences.

Ready to make an impact?

Apply today or visit https://www.abb.com to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

jobs.title: Production Supervisor (80 -100%)
jobs.description:
At ABB, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

This position reports to:

Production Manager

__

Your role and responsibilities

The ABB Quartino plant is part of the Critical Power Business Line, where we develop and produce UPS systems designed to stabilize and ensure continuity of power grid supply at our global customers' facilities. The strong market expansion and expected volume increase in the coming years require an expansion of our production organization. The production structure is organized in Integrated Production Units (IPUs), divided by product type. Each IPU is autonomously organized so it can independently coordinate all the resources needed to ensure the required production outputs, while maintaining the expected quality and costs.

For one of our IPUs we are looking for a Production Supervisor that will report to the IPU Leader and will work alongside the other IPUs coordinating the assigned product lines. You will ensure efficient and cost-effective production results that meet order demand, specifications, quality, and planning targets through the coordination and management of plant equipment, personnel, and production processes (quantity, quality, costs, and proper productivity level).

The work model for the role is: #LI- onsite

You will be mainly accountable for:

  • Operational & Production Management: Coordinate all production resources—personnel, equipment, and production area—to meet key operational targets including output, On-Time Delivery, and First Pass Yield, while actively participating in projects to optimize performance and working capital.

  • Team Leadership & Development: Lead, guide, and develop a high-performing production team by ensuring proper staffing and skills, providing coaching and motivation, and fostering a collaborative culture in line with HR policies and ABB's core values.

  • HSE & Compliance Ownership: Take full accountability for the Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) of your team, assessing risks and implementing plans to ensure strict compliance with all ABB integrity standards, local regulations, and the code of conduct.

  • Continuous Improvement & Training: Drive a culture of continuous improvement by collaborating with Operational Excellence teams to integrate lean manufacturing and Kaizen principles, while ensuring the implementation of necessary safety, technical, and process-related training programs for your organization.

Qualifications for the role

  • A degree in a technical discipline such as Electronics, Mechanics, or a related field.

  • A deep understanding of end-to-end production processes and logistics within a manufacturing environment.

  • A minimum of 5 years of proven experience in a production environment, including significant time in a Team Leader or supervisory capacity.

  • At least 3 years of hands-on leadership experience, with direct responsibility for managing and developing production teams or shop-floor personnel.

  • Demonstrated expertise in operational resource planning, including the organization of personnel, production equipment, and shift schedules.

  • Strong leadership and motivational skills, combined with a proven ability to manage conflicts and foster a collaborative team environment.

  • Fluency in Italian is required; a good working knowledge of English is a valuable asset

More about us

ABB Smart Power provides energy distribution solutions for data centers, industrial and manufacturing plants, critical infrastructure and commercial buildings. The Division’s technical teams work closely with industry partners, delivering advanced solutions that support rapid growth, energy transition, and sustainability objectives. The Division’s portfolio includes industrial circuit breakers, low-voltage systems, motor starting applications, and safety devices like switches and relays. Its Power Protection unit supports the world’s largest data center companies with advanced energy-efficient UPS solutions. The Division’s ABB Ability™ Energy Manager provides a scalable, easy-to-use platform that helps organizations save energy and reduce CO2 emissions.

We look forward to receiving your application.

Learn more about ABB at www.abb.com.
Read our Data Privacy Statement.

Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences.

Ready to make an impact?

Apply today or visit https://www.abb.com to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

Qualifiche per il ruolo - Una laurea in una disciplina tecnica come Elettronica, Meccanica, o un campo correlato. - Una profonda comprensione dei processi produttivi end-to-end e della logistica in un ambiente di produzione. - Un minimo di 5 anni di esperienza comprovata in un ambiente di produzione, compreso il tempo significativo in un Team Leader o capacità di supervisione. - No. Almeno 3 anni di esperienza di leadership pratica, con responsabilità diretta per la gestione e lo sviluppo di team di produzione o personale di laboratorio. - Competenza dimostrata nella pianificazione delle risorse operative, inclusa l'organizzazione del personale, delle attrezzature di produzione e dei turni. - Forte leadership e competenze motivazionali, combinate con una comprovata capacità di gestire i conflitti e promuovere un ambiente di team collaborativo. ABB Smart Power offre soluzioni di distribuzione energetica per data center, impianti industriali e produttivi, infrastrutture critiche e edifici commerciali. I team tecnici della Divisione lavorano a stretto contatto con i partner del settore, offrendo soluzioni avanzate che supportano obiettivi di crescita rapida, transizione energetica e sostenibilità. Il portafoglio della Divisione comprende interruttori industriali, sistemi a bassa tensione, applicazioni di avviamento motore e dispositivi di sicurezza come interruttori e relè. La sua unità Power Protection supporta le più grandi aziende di data center del mondo con soluzioni UPS ad alta efficienza energetica. ABB AbilityTM Energy Manager offre una piattaforma scalabile e facile da usare che consente alle organizzazioni di risparmiare energia e ridurre le emissioni di CO2. Non vediamo l'ora di ricevere la vostra domanda. Maggiori informazioni su ABB su www.abb.com . Leggi la nostra Informativa sulla privacy dei dati . Apprezziamo le persone provenienti da diversi background. Potrebbe essere la tua storia? Applicare oggi o visitare www.abb.com per saperne di più su di noi e conoscere l'impatto delle nostre soluzioni in tutto il mondo.

descriptionByLocale.fr: Chez ABB, nous aidons les industries à dépasser - plus maigre et plus propre. Ici, le progrès est une attente - pour vous, votre équipe, et le monde. En tant que leader mondial du marché, nous vous donnerons ce dont vous avez besoin pour que cela se produise. Il n'a pas toujours été facile, la croissance prend du grain. Mais à ABB, vous ne fuirez jamais seul. Courez ce qui dirige le monde. Ce poste relève de : Gestionnaire de la production Votre rôle et vos responsabilités L'usine ABB Quartino fait partie de Critical Power Business Line, où nous développons et produisons des systèmes UPS conçus pour stabiliser et assurer la continuité de l'approvisionnement en réseau électrique dans les installations de nos clients mondiaux. La forte expansion du marché et l'augmentation prévue du volume dans les années à venir exigent une expansion de notre organisation de production. La structure de production est organisée en unités de production intégrées, divisée par type de produit. Chaque UIP est organisée de manière autonome afin de pouvoir coordonner de manière indépendante toutes les ressources nécessaires pour assurer les produits de production requis, tout en maintenant la qualité et les coûts escomptés. Pour l'une de nos UIP, nous sommes à la recherche d'un superviseur de la production qui relèvera du chef de l'UIP et travaillera avec les autres UIP en coordonnant les gammes de produits assignées. Vous assurerez des résultats de production efficaces et rentables qui répondent à la demande, aux spécifications, à la qualité et aux objectifs de planification grâce à la coordination et à la gestion des équipements, du personnel et des processus de production (quantité, qualité, coûts et niveau de productivité approprié). Le modèle de travail pour le rôle est: #LI- sur place Vous serez principalement responsable : - de la gestion opérationnelle et de la production : Coordonner toutes les ressources de production – du personnel, de l'équipement et de la zone de production – afin d'atteindre les objectifs opérationnels clés, y compris la production, la livraison en temps opportun et le rendement de First Pass, tout en participant activement à des projets visant à optimiser les performances et le fonds de roulement. - Leadership et perfectionnement de l'équipe : Diriger, guider et développer une équipe de production hautement performante en assurant une dotation et des compétences adéquates, en fournissant du coaching et de la motivation et en favorisant une culture de collaboration conforme aux politiques des RH et aux valeurs fondamentales de l'ABB. - HSE & Compliance Ownership : Rendre pleinement compte de la santé, de la sécurité et de l'environnement (HSE) de votre équipe, évaluer les risques et mettre en oeuvre des plans pour assurer le strict respect de toutes les normes d'intégrité ABB, de la réglementation locale et du code de conduite. - Amélioration et formation continues : Conduire une culture d'amélioration continue en collaborant avec des équipes d'excellence opérationnelle afin d'intégrer la fabrication maigre et les principes Kaizen, tout en assurant la mise en oeuvre des programmes de formation nécessaires en matière de sécurité, de technique et de processus pour votre organisation. Qualifications pour le rôle - Diplôme dans une discipline technique comme l'électronique, la mécanique ou un domaine connexe. - Une compréhension approfondie des processus de production de bout en bout et de la logistique dans un environnement de fabrication. - Un minimum de 5 ans d'expérience prouvée dans un environnement de production, y compris un temps important dans un chef d'équipe ou une capacité de supervision. - Oui. Au moins 3 ans d'expérience pratique en leadership, avec la responsabilité directe de gérer et de développer des équipes de production ou du personnel d'atelier. - Expertise démontrée dans la planification des ressources opérationnelles, y compris l'organisation du personnel, du matériel de production et des horaires de travail. - Des compétences solides en leadership et en motivation, associées à une capacité éprouvée de gérer les conflits et de favoriser un environnement d'équipe collaborative. - Une bonne connaissance pratique de l'anglais est un atout précieux.En savoir plus sur nous ABB Smart Power fournit des solutions de distribution d'énergie pour les centres de données, les usines industrielles et manufacturières, les infrastructures critiques et les bâtiments commerciaux. jobs.title: Technical Support Engineer (80-100%)
jobs.description:
At ABB, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

This position reports to:

Service Team Leader

__

Your role and responsibilities

In this role, you will have the opportunity to provide technical assistance to internal and external customers and ensure timely and cost-effective support in accordance with guidelines. Each day, you will contribute to customer satisfaction by executing support work according to customer expectations. You will also showcase your skill in customer service by ensuring customer retention through delivering high-quality customer experience

The work model for the role is: #LI-Hybrid

You will be mainly responsible for

  • Providing hardware support for ABB UPS (Uninterruptible Power Source) and PCS (Power Conditioning) products via our ticketing system, phone, or email, including supporting partners and subsidiaries through the 24/7 Diagnostic Centre rotation.

  • Performing complex system analysis for ABB UPS and PCS products and documenting your findings for relevant stakeholders.

  • Occasionally traveling to support customers, partners, and ABB subsidiaries with complex installations (such as large data centers), auditing service operations, and developing the competencies of local teams. As well as providing technical training on UPS and PCS products for customers and service engineers as required.

  • Interacting with other departments, such as Quality and R&D, to address product issues and drive continuous product improvement.

Qualifications for the role

  • A Bachelor's degree, Diploma, or higher academic qualification in Electrical or Electronic Engineering.

  • Several years of hands-on experience in commissioning, after-sales technical support, and advanced diagnostics of UPS, PCS, or energy storage solutions. Experience with ABB products is highly beneficial.

  • Demonstrated experience in commissioning and troubleshooting mission-critical applications such as data centers, high-volume industrial facilities, and critical infrastructure installations.

  • Proficiency in both verbal and written English communication (B2 level or higher). Knowledge of any other language is a welcome asset.

  • The ability to write clear and concise technical documents, reports, and field support procedures.

  • A good working knowledge of the MS Office package.

  • A willingness to engage directly with clients and teams worldwide, with a travel commitment of up to 15%.

  • Certification in quality and electrical safety standards (e.g., Six Sigma) is highly beneficial.

Some benefits

  • Flexible working hours

  • Hybrid working

  • ABB - SWIBECO benefits

  • More interesting ABB benefits



More about us

ABB Smart Power provides energy distribution solutions for data centers, industrial and manufacturing plants, critical infrastructure and commercial buildings. The Division’s technical teams work closely with industry partners, delivering advanced solutions that support rapid growth, energy transition, and sustainability objectives. The Division’s portfolio includes industrial circuit breakers, low-voltage systems, motor starting applications, and safety devices like switches and relays. Its Power Protection unit supports the world’s largest data center companies with advanced energy-efficient UPS solutions. The Division’s ABB Ability™ Energy Manager provides a scalable, easy-to-use platform that helps organizations save energy and reduce CO2 emissions.

Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences.

Ready to make an impact?

Apply today or visit https://www.abb.com to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

Inoltre, fornire formazione tecnica sui prodotti UPS e PCS per i clienti e gli ingegneri di servizio, se necessario. - Interagire con altri dipartimenti, come Qualità e R&D, per affrontare problemi relativi ai prodotti e promuovere miglioramenti continui dei prodotti. ## Requisiti - Una laurea triennale, diploma o qualifica accademica superiore in Ingegneria Elettrica o Elettronica. - Diversi anni di esperienza pratica nella messa in servizio, nel supporto tecnico post-vendita e nella diagnostica avanzata di UPS, PCS o soluzioni di accumulo energetico. L'esperienza con i prodotti ABB è altamente beneficiaria. - Esperienza dimostrata nella messa in servizio e nella risoluzione dei problemi di applicazioni mission-critical come data center, impianti industriali ad alto volume e installazioni di infrastrutture critiche. - Padronanza della comunicazione verbale e scritta in inglese (livello B2 o superiore). La conoscenza di qualsiasi altra lingua è un bene accetto. - La capacità di scrivere documenti tecnici, report e procedure di supporto sul campo chiari e concisi. - Buona conoscenza del pacchetto MS Office. - La volontà di impegnarsi direttamente con i clienti e le squadre in tutto il mondo, con un impegno di viaggio fino al 15%. - Certificazione in standard di qualità e sicurezza elettrica (ad esempio, Six Sigma) è altamente beneficiaria. ## Cosa offriamo - Orari di lavoro flessibili - Lavoro ibrido - Benefici ABB - SWIBECO - Altri interessanti benefici ABB ## Chi siamo ABB Smart Power fornisce soluzioni di distribuzione di energia per data center, impianti industriali e manifatturieri, infrastrutture critiche e edifici commerciali. L'unità di Protezione della Potenza supporta le più grandi aziende di data center del mondo con soluzioni UPS avanzate e efficienti in termini di energia. L'ABB Ability™ Energy Manager della Divisione è una piattaforma scalabile e facile da usare che aiuta le organizzazioni a risparmiare energia e ridurre le emissioni di CO2. Valutiamo le persone provenienti da diversi background. Potrebbe essere la tua storia? jobs.title: (80 - 100%) Warehouse/Internal Logistics Supervisor
jobs.description:
At ABB, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

This position reports to:

Warehouse/Internal Logistics Manager

__

In this role you will have the opportunity to lead and continuously improve warehouse and internal logistics operations in a manufacturing environment, ensuring safe, efficient and reliable material flows that support production and business objectives. Each day, you will coordinate inbound and outbound logistics activities, supervise warehouse operations, and ensure inventory accuracy while driving operational excellence.

You will also showcase your expertise by identifying and implementing continuous improvement initiatives, optimizing warehouse layout and capacity, and collaborating cross functionally to enhance service levels and efficiency.

You will join a collaborative, engaged, and performance driven team, where continuous improvement, ownership, and teamwork are strongly encouraged, and where you will be able to thrive and make a tangible impact.

Main stakeholders are Production, Planning, Quality, Engineering, Procurement, Supply Chain, Finance, and External Logistics Providers.

The work model for the role is: Onsite

You will be mainly accountable for:

  • Coding and preparing proper receipt/dispatch paperwork.
  • Ensuring maintenance of equipment and facilities and identifying and recommending technical equipment and facility improvements as necessary
  • Optimizing warehouse capacity, inventory, and floor utilization and supervising and assisting in daily warehouse and shipping operations
  • Recommending measures to improve service quality, increase departmental efficiency, and boost the performance of work crews and equipment; and ensuring stock accuracy by conducting periodic stock takes and/or cycle counting

Qualifications for the role:

  • You are highly skilled in warehouse and internal logistics operations, working with ERP systems, inventory management, lean logistics, and continuous improvement methodologies within the manufacturing / industrial automation sector
  • You have at least 5 years of experience in warehouse, logistics, or supply chain operations, preferably in an industrial or manufacturing environment
  • You possess strong knowledge of ERP systems (SAP) and WMS (Warehouse management system) tools, with a solid understanding of inventory control and stock accuracy processes.
  • You are innovative and proactive, with strong problem solving and continuous improvement capabilities, and a handson leadership approach
  • You have proven experience leading or supervising operational teams, ensuring safety, performance, and adherence to procedures
  • You hold a degree or equivalent experience in Logistics, Supply Chain, Industrial Engineering, or a related field
  • You are at ease communicating in Italian and English

What's in it for you?

  • Opportunity to work in a high technology manufacturing environment within a growing and strategic product line
  • High level of ownership and visibility, with real impact on operational performance and efficiency
  • Continuous learning and development opportunities in logistics, automation, and operational excellence

More about us (Mandatory)
ABB is a global technology leader in electrification and automation, enabling a more sustainable and resource-efficient future. By connecting its engineering and digitalization expertise, ABB helps industries run at high performance, while becoming more efficient, productive and sustainable so they outperform. At ABB, we call this ‘Engineered to Outrun’. The company has over 140 years of history and around 110,000 employees worldwide. ABB’s shares are listed on the SIX Swiss Exchange (ABBN) and Nasdaq Stockholm (ABB). www.abb.com

At ABB, we have the clear goal of driving diversity and inclusion across all dimensions: gender, LGBTQ+, abilities, ethnicity, generations, etc. Together, we are embarking in a journey where each one of us, individually and collectively, welcomes and celebrates individual differences.

Guide the future. This is where innovation accelerates, industries are reimagined, and your impact powers the world ahead. Run What Runs the World.

#ABBCareers

#RunwithABB

#Runwhatrunstheworld

Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences.

Ready to make an impact?

Apply today or visit https://www.abb.com to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.
